title: [ImportVerilog] Fix Bug in Packed Array Lvalue

features.name: packed_array_concatenation_lvalue
features.description: Code should include a concatenation lvalue where elements are multi-dimensional packed arrays, such as {a, b} = x where a and b are logic [7:0][63:0]. This triggers a bug in CIRCT ImportVerilog where packed array lvalues in concatenations are mishandled, causing the assignment to be dropped.
features.snippet:
logic [7:0][63:0] a, b;
always_comb {a, b} = x;

features.name: nested_packed_array_assignment
features.description: Code should include assignment between nested packed arrays where a flat packed bit vector (e.g., logic [1023:0]) is assigned to a concatenation of nested packed arrays (e.g., two logic [7:0][63:0] variables). This exercises the packed array wire/reg type coercion in lvalue positions.

features.name: always_comb_concatenation_assignment
features.description: Code should include an always_comb block with a concatenation assignment where the right-hand side is a packed array or logic vector. The bug manifests when ImportVerilog processes always_comb with concatenation lvalues containing packed array variables.
